reviews.review_title: Not as scary as the trailer
reviews.review_description: But still lots of tense moments and jump scares. I'm still trying to figure out how each monster works but it's fun and fast - about as long as I can manage to be in VR. It's got a fun lobby and is super worth jumping in with a few friends to discover and run around.

developer_response.body: Scary is a sliding-scale, right? We hope there's a little bit for every thrill-seeker. Thanks for jumping in!

reviews.review_description:
It's interesting how the success of extraction games like Lethal Company and other party games like Fall Guys has had such an impact on the industry. I've always wanted to know what it would be like to play something like this in VR. 
It has a good atmosphere and the enemies have already given me a few scares. It's definitely better to play with others. Although I still don't fully understand how all the items work, I assume the intention is for players to discover it on their own; but it wouldn't hurt to add a guide for it.

developer_response.body: this is great feedback - we've got a tutorial to the left of the path when you first spawn and we're always looking at ways to improve player experience. We like playing with friends as well so if you ever need some, jump in Discord!

reviews.review_description: I barely play VR, but my friends made me buy INWIGO and it's honestly a blast. Perfect horror game to play together. It’s creepy but we ended up laughing so much because of how chaotic it gets. But man, the monsters are no joke. One snuck up on me and I literally screamed out loud in my room. If you want a mix of genuine scares and pure fun with friends, definitely get it. 10/10

developer_response.body: Hey Michael - bummed that your friends made you BUY the game since it's free! But glad you got in there and had a good time. Come back and play us again, and don't fall for that "you have to pay to play" trick!

reviews.review_title: Awesome art style and spooky fun
reviews.review_description: Really cool art style, and the gameplay is super fun. Very family friendly kind of spooky (think “Something Wicked This Way Comes” type of scares). The atmosphere is on point!

developer_response.body: That movie/book is an oldie but a goodie and one we're glad to hear referenced when talking about INWIGO. A large part of game dev is pulling inspiration from mediums and projects that have influenced us. Thanks for the feedback!

reviews.review_title: If you like R.E.P.O. or Lethal Company
reviews.review_description: Great extraction game, The house gets angrier the more you take, it gets a little crazy once your pockets are full. Much better with friends

developer_response.body: Honored to be listed among those two great games! We're currently looking to add more ways for you to carry more loot. And we agree - hunting, or haunting, is always better when there are two of you to scare!

reviews.review_description: Definitely gets the blood flowing as you try to escape the monsters, playing with a group is the best, you can use your friends as bait, lol.  Remember, you don't have to be faster than the bear, just faster than your friends!

developer_response.body: Friends as bait? We see you @eastmad1 and we're not mad. Strategy is everything!

reviews.review_description: It's pretty cool. It has some good scares, the atmosphere is really well done and it feels super immersive. The lobby is fun, I really recommend it play with friends, even tho it have a solo play mode.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for the feedback @deibi! Glad you like the atmosphere - we're really proud of the artists and what they've been able to bring to life for gamers. If you ever need more friends to hunt with, jump into our Discord and holler.

reviews.review_description:
I still need to get what all the monsters do, and I didnt know I could hide from them, but it's fun to play with friends, solo is harder but still fun haha
I like the style and visuals

developer_response.body: Love that you love the art - we dig it, too. Thanks for playing and good luck on figuring out how to engage with each monster. A small hint: the guy with the torch may not be able to see you but can hear everything! Good luck!

reviews.review_description:
If you've played VR before, you know horror is usually a given, it's easy to scare players when a game throws something right in their face. But not many games take advantage of VR the way Inwigo does.
You can feel the tension the moment you step inside the house. The environment has a distinct style and a genuinely creepy atmosphere, and the character designs are just as memorable as the carnival and the house itself. Every creature feels unique and fits perfectly within the game's unsettling world. The audio design makes the house feel alive and threatening, constantly keeping you looking over your shoulder.
And oh boy, when a monster starts chasing you, that's when things get real.
I can't recommend this game enough. Do yourself a favor and download it now!

developer_response.body: Glad you liked the game and thanks for the feedback! We tried to keep the scares intentional and really build the tension just as much with what isn't there as is.

reviews.review_description:
It looked like fun in the trailer but in game it's very disappointing.  The graphics are pretty primitive and the VR interactions are janky.

Opening drawers and cabinet doors feels really clunky. 

It also has the strangest throwing mechanic I've ever encountered in VR.  It's difficult to describe, you'll find out when you try to throw a basketball.

I met some people and we formed a party. The game transitioned to the mansion but I was alone. After a few minutes I hit a glitch and became embedded in a chest of drawers and couldn't get free. I quit the game and uninstalled it.

developer_response.body: Jioxx - this is really valuable feedback. One of the reasons we launched into early access is to get it into the hands of more VR players who could speak thoughtfully to the mechanics and overall gameplay. Our whole goal with early access is to give our team of scrappy devs the opportunity to improve things like mechanics, game loop, environment mapping... a whole laundry list. We want a game you WANT to play. So we hope, this is just a break. And if you're open to it, we'd love your continued feedback. If you're open to it, just join our Discord and drop our team a DM. We'd love to chat more! And if nothing else, thanks for jumping in and taking the time to write a review.

reviews.review_description:
Was looting and scooting in a kitchen then a creepy doll kept following me, it eventually started running at me and strangled me to death, I got too greedy! 
10/10 would die in that house again! Fun silly horror game would recommend if you like repo or leathal company!

developer_response.body: Good company to be in! Strangled by a small doll...we've all been there, amiright? Greed is the game when gold is the goal. Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_title: Great atmosphere and plenty of scares
reviews.review_description: I wasn't expecting to get so invested, but the game does a great job building tension. The house feels creepy, the monsters are unpredictable, and every run feels a little different. Still learning all the mechanics, but discovering them with friends has been a lot of fun.

developer_response.body: We've got a really cool generative map tool that our devs custom built that is pretty awesome! No two runs are going to feel the same. Thanks for the review - hopefully we can keep on impressing you.
